paper_title: Growth Inhibition and Chemical Analysis of Antibiotics on Algal Species

methods.description: Procedure for assessing the growth inhibition of algal species by antibiotics following the OECD Guideline 201.

steps.description: All glassware and stoppers used in the tests were autoclaved at 121°C for 30 min before use.
steps.searchable_summary: autoclave glassware and stoppers

steps.description: Triplicates of six concentrations of each antibiotic and a negative control were prepared in the corresponding culture medium solution.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare antibiotic concentrations and controls

steps.description: After addition of the antibiotic, samples sterilized by filtration (pore size 0.2 lm) were added into a 25-mL vial, and precultured algal cells grown in the logarithmic phase were inoculated into the vial to obtain 15-mL solution with an initial density 5 9 10 5 cells mL -1.
steps.searchable_summary: inoculate algal cells into antibiotic solutions

steps.description: Following the inoculation, these vials were capped with air-permeable stoppers made of cotton and muslin.
steps.searchable_summary: cap vials with air-permeable stoppers

steps.description: All operations were undertaken in a sterilized chamber, and the vials were then incubated for 4 days under the same conditions as the cultures.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate vials in sterilized chamber

steps.description: Cell density in each sample was measured at 24-h intervals using UV-Visible spectrophotometry.
steps.searchable_summary: measure cell density using spectrophotometry

steps.description: Cell density was calculated from a calibration curve of known cell density counted by a haemocytometer against adsorption measured by an ultraviolet and visible (UV-Vis) spectrophotometry.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate cell density from calibration curve

steps.description: Growth inhibition of each alga was calculated from the yield of algal cell density in each treatment after 4-days exposure.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate growth inhibition from cell density yield

steps.description: The percent inhibition in yield (% I y ) was calculated by Eq. 1 (OECD 2011).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percent inhibition in yield

steps.description: Two-step experiments including range-finding and determination were conducted in growth inhibition tests.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct range-finding and determination experiments

steps.description: The prepared concentrations of antibiotics before the test were confirmed by chemical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: confirm antibiotic concentrations by chemical analysis

steps.description: Samples with the highest and lowest concentrations were analysed again after the test to determine the antibiotic stability.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze antibiotic stability post-test

steps.description: Recovery was defined as the antibiotic concentration in algal solution after 4-days exposure compared with the initial concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate recovery of antibiotic concentration

steps.description: In cases where recoveries were less than 80%, dissipation followed first-order kinetics and a dissipation rate constant (k) was estimated.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate dissipation rate constant for low recovery

steps.description: The k was then applied in Eq. 3 to estimate the time-weighted average concentration (TWAC) over t days.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate TWAC using dissipation rate constant

steps.description: Concentration-response curves were obtained by fitting regression analysis of sigmoidal functions embedded in the Sigma plot software version 12.0.
steps.searchable_summary: fit concentration-response curves using regression analysis

methods.description: Procedures for confirming antibiotic concentrations in exposure solutions using high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC).

steps.description: Concentrations of the antibiotics in the exposure solutions were confirmed using high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) using an Agilent 1100 with C18 Supelco Discovery column.
steps.searchable_summary: confirm antibiotic concentrations using HPLC

steps.description: Tylosin and trimethoprim were analysed using a 24-min gradient method with methanol and a buffer as the mobile phase.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze tylosin and trimethoprim using gradient method

steps.description: Lincomycin was analysed by an isocratic method using 0.1 % formic acid plus acetonitrile.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze lincomycin using isocratic method

steps.description: Quantification was performed from a calibration curve constructed from standards of each antibiotic.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify antibiotics using calibration curve

steps.description: For measuring low concentration solutions, solid phase extraction (SPE) was used to concentrate the samples prior to analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: concentrate samples using SPE for low concentration analysis

methods.method_name: Toxicity Test Analysis Based on Growth
methods.description: Analysis of the effects of antibiotics on the growth of algal species, including determination of EC 50 values.

steps.description: Determine the 96 h EC 50 for tylosin for growth inhibition of P. subcapitata.
steps.searchable_summary: determine EC 50 for tylosin on P. subcapitata

steps.description: Compare obtained EC 50 values with previously reported values.
steps.searchable_summary: compare EC 50 values with literature

steps.description: Analyze potential effects of trimethoprim on various algal species.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze trimethoprim effects on algal species

methods.method_name: Toxicity Test Analysis Based on Photosynthesis and Endpoint Sensitivity Comparison
methods.description: Comparison of photosynthesis and growth as endpoints for assessing antibiotic toxicity on algal species.

steps.description: Determine EC 50 values for chlorophytes based on photosynthesis as an endpoint.
steps.searchable_summary: determine EC 50 for chlorophytes based on photosynthesis

steps.description: Compare sensitivity of photosynthesis and growth endpoints for cyanobacteria and diatoms.
steps.searchable_summary: compare endpoint sensitivity for cyanobacteria and diatoms

steps.description: Suggest inclusion of oxygen evolution rate measurements as an additional endpoint for chlorophytes.
steps.searchable_summary: suggest oxygen evolution rate as endpoint for chlorophytes
